item.title: A Woman Sold as a Child Used Social Media to Find Her Trafficker
item.description: Yang Niuhua was taken from her family in China in 1995 and sold by trafficker Yu Huaying when Yang was five years old. Decades later, Yang documented her search for her birth family on social media, ultimately filing a police report that led to Yu's arrest in 2022. Yu was convicted of trafficking 17 children between 1993 and 2003 and was sentenced to death. She was executed in February 2025.

media:content.media:title: A Woman Sold as a Child Used Social Media to Find Her Trafficker
media:content.media:description: Yang Niuhua was taken from her family in China in 1995 and sold by trafficker Yu Huaying when Yang was five years old. Decades later, Yang documented her search for her birth family on social media, ultimately filing a police report that led to Yu's arrest in 2022. Yu was convicted of trafficking 17 children between 1993 and 2003 and was sentenced to death. She was executed in February 2025.

item.title: Zachary Witman Was Convicted of Killing His Brother and Released After a Plea Deal
item.description: In October 1998, 13-year-old Gregory Witman was found dead inside the family home in New Freedom, Pennsylvania, shortly after arriving home from school. His 15-year-old brother Zachary, who had stayed home that day, called 911. Investigators found the murder weapon and gloves buried in the backyard. Zachary was convicted of first-degree murder in 2003 as an adult and sentenced to life in prison without parole, a sentence he maintained was unjust. His original conviction was later voided after it was determined his attorney had withheld a plea offer in 2002. In 2018, Zachary pleaded guilty to third-degree murder and was released on parole in May 2019 after serving approximately 20 years.

media:content.media:title: Zachary Witman Was Convicted of Killing His Brother and Released After a Plea Deal
media:content.media:description: In October 1998, 13-year-old Gregory Witman was found dead inside the family home in New Freedom, Pennsylvania, shortly after arriving home from school. His 15-year-old brother Zachary, who had stayed home that day, called 911. Investigators found the murder weapon and gloves buried in the backyard. Zachary was convicted of first-degree murder in 2003 as an adult and sentenced to life in prison without parole, a sentence he maintained was unjust. His original conviction was later voided after it was determined his attorney had withheld a plea offer in 2002. In 2018, Zachary pleaded guilty to third-degree murder and was released on parole in May 2019 after serving approximately 20 years.
